note: Spec-owned vectors. Each vector is recomputable from `inputs` alone; `expected` is the outcome a correct reviewer must reach FROM the inputs. A reference impl imports nothing from this bench and recomputes from inputs. The bench scores reviewability of evidence PER AXIS, with NO aggregate score.

axes.sufficiency: a valid record is not sufficient without coverage
axes.source_class_ceiling: who attested caps the maximum supportable claim
axes.recompute: the verdict is recomputed from records, not asserted; prose is ignored
axes.format_equivalence: shape-different but semantically-same records group as equivalent
axes.tamper_fail_closed: any digest mismatch or missing digest fails closed
axes.incomplete_visibility: absent / not-checked observation surfaces as incomplete, never clean

vectors.property: valid record + full coverage is sufficient
vectors.non_claims: does not assert the action occurred, only that the evidence supports a conclusion

vectors.property: valid record with no coverage is NOT sufficient (absent != clean)
vectors.non_claims: incomplete is not a failure of the record, it is a gap in coverage

vectors.property: an invalid record cannot be sufficient regardless of coverage
vectors.non_claims: validity is necessary but not sufficient

vectors.property: a self-report may carry an asserted-level claim
vectors.non_claims: within-ceiling is not proof the action occurred

vectors.property: a self-report cannot reach independently_confirmed
vectors.non_claims: the claim is capped, not the record rejected

vectors.property: a third-party observation may carry independently_confirmed
vectors.non_claims: ceiling ordering is RGE-Bench's proposed ranking, not a standard

vectors.property: observed effects within declared -> match
vectors.non_claims: static/record-level, not runtime behavior

vectors.property: an observed effect outside declared -> mismatch
vectors.non_claims: mismatch is an evidence signal, not a verdict on intent

vectors.property: prose is not evidence: rewording the description does not change the verdict
vectors.non_claims: the recompute ignores the natural-language description

vectors.property: same envelope shape + same semantics -> equivalent
vectors.non_claims: equivalence is over semantic fields, not raw bytes

vectors.property: DIFFERENT envelope shapes + same semantics -> still equivalent (cross-shape normalization)
vectors.non_claims: the envelope shape is metadata, excluded from equivalence

vectors.property: same shape + different semantics -> distinct
vectors.non_claims: a real semantic difference must split

vectors.property: stored digest equals recomputed -> accepted
vectors.non_claims: acceptance is integrity only, not authenticity

vectors.property: any digest mismatch -> rejected (fail closed)
vectors.non_claims: rejection is integrity-only, not an attribution claim

vectors.property: missing stored digest -> rejected (fail closed, never degrade to a soft pass)
vectors.non_claims: absence of a digest is not a pass

vectors.property: an observed surface -> observed
vectors.non_claims: observed means watched, not that the result is good

vectors.property: a not-checked surface surfaces as incomplete, never clean
vectors.non_claims: not_checked is a visible gap, not a pass

vectors.property: an absent observation surfaces as incomplete
vectors.non_claims: absent != clean

vectors.property: coding agent: observed file/command/tool effects all within declared scope -> match

inputs.description: Edited the target module and ran the tests.

vectors.non_claims: record-level scope check, not a runtime-behavior guarantee

vectors.property: coding agent: an edit to a file outside declared scope -> mismatch
vectors.non_claims: mismatch is an evidence signal, not a verdict on intent

vectors.property: coding agent: an MCP tool call outside declared scope -> mismatch
vectors.non_claims: the tool surface is part of the declared scope

vectors.property: coding agent: an authorized run whose observed effect exceeds scope still -> mismatch (authorization does not upgrade)

inputs.description: Authorized refactor of the target module.

vectors.non_claims: a delegation authorizes; observation confirms -- authorization is not conformance

vectors.property: coding agent: the agent's own log (producer_reported) cannot reach independently_confirmed
vectors.non_claims: the claim is capped, not the record rejected

vectors.property: coding agent: a boundary-observed run may carry observed_in_path
vectors.non_claims: ceiling ordering is RGE-Bench's proposed ranking, not a standard

vectors.property: coding agent: an evidence pack whose body was altered after hashing -> rejected
vectors.non_claims: rejection is integrity-only, not an attribution claim

vectors.property: coding agent: a pack with no content hash -> rejected (fail closed)
vectors.non_claims: absence of a content hash is not a pass

vectors.property: coding agent: the network surface was not observed -> incomplete (absent != clean)
vectors.non_claims: network egress is high-blast-radius; an unobserved surface is a gap, not a pass

vectors.property: coding agent: the shell-command surface was not checked -> incomplete
vectors.non_claims: not_checked is a visible gap, not a pass

vectors.property: coding agent: the MCP-tool surface was observed -> observed
vectors.non_claims: observed means the surface was watched, not that the result is good

vectors.property: coding agent: a valid pack with all core surfaces observed is sufficient
vectors.non_claims: sufficient supports a conclusion; it does not assert the change is correct

vectors.property: coding agent: a valid pack missing network coverage is NOT sufficient
vectors.non_claims: incomplete is a coverage gap, not a defect in the record

vectors.property: coding agent: a malformed pack (e.g. missing network policy) cannot be sufficient even with full coverage
vectors.non_claims: validity is necessary but not sufficient
